blockingIssues.description: Missing implementation. The feature requires routing tool calls from the WebSocket to the React Renderer and then via IPC to Electron Main. No frontend code was modified to handle incoming `tool_call` WebSocket messages or pass them to Electron Main.

appliedUpdates.description: Added midscene.md documenting how to configure @midscene/computer for Gemini models.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how features that span both backend and frontend boundaries should be handled. If assigned to a specialized worker (like backend-worker), they may artificially limit themselves and fail to complete the feature.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature 'midscene-tool-call' required routing from Python backend to React Renderer to Electron Main, but was assigned to backend-worker. The worker only implemented the backend portion and ignored the frontend portion.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Provide workers with standardized procedures or commands for running multiple background services (e.g. backend and frontend) concurrently so they can perform end-to-end verification without struggling with unmanaged background processes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Worker in 'midscene-tool-call' abandoned manual end-to-end verification because they struggled to run backend and frontend concurrently and capture logs.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker had to add 'dist-electron' and 'test-results' to ESLint globalIgnores.
rejectedObservations.reason: This code change is specific to frontend linting setup and was already applied by the worker, no further guidance or library update is needed.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker had to remove unused imports from 'electron.spec.ts' to fix linting errors.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a one-off code hygiene fix, not a reusable pattern or systemic issue.
